output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 5/5.
Batch 1: Implemented T1 in the video-editor tree. `rowsToConfig()` now serializes timeline timing fields with a shared 4-decimal policy and computes hold durations from one rounded start/end pair so repeated rematerialization keeps grouped interior boundaries stable. Added a repeated round-trip regression that proves adjacent grouped hold clips preserve a 2.3334 shared boundary with no inward jump, gap, or overlap across six cycles. Checkpointed batch progress to `.megaplan/plans/finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Completed batch 2. `TimelineCanvas` now treats `lostpointercapture` as the same resize-finalization path as pointer-up, with a regression proving preview overrides clear, capture is released, pending ops drain, `onActionResizeEnd` fires, and interaction-end listeners run. The type/migration layer is now soft-tag-only on the exported `PinnedShotGroup` shape: legacy `start` and `children` were removed from the type, snapshot bounds gained optional `start`/`end`, `repairConfig()` converts projection-shaped groups to ordered `clipIds` only, and serialization tests assert legacy fields are never re-emitted. Checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Completed batch 3’s scoped soft-tag rewrites. `usePinnedShotGroups.ts`, `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`, `useExternalDrop.ts`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` now commit soft-tag group state through live rows/meta/clip-order updates instead of projection-model shims, and the affected hook tests were realigned to `clipIds` plus snapshot bounds. Wrote a batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628/execution_batch_3.json`. Verification across the full batch target file set now passes for `useShotGroups`, `useClipDrag`, `useExternalDrop`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement`; the only remaining failures are two pre-existing `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests in `useClipEditing.test.ts`, which exercise untouched code outside this batch’s modified paths.
Batch 4: Completed batch 4's compatibility-layer cleanup. The deprecated pinned-group projection shims are removed from `pinned-group-projection.ts`, the remaining soft-tag tests no longer build runtime groups through legacy `start`/`children` fixtures, and the targeted cleanup sweep now passes for `timeline-save-utils`, `serialize`, `useClipDrag`, `useClipEditing`, and `useShotGroups`. Wrote a batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Completed batch 5 verification. A throwaway repro test under `src/tools/video-editor` reproduced the grouped-boundary drift and lost-pointer-capture stuck-preview symptoms in legacy `before` mode, confirmed the fixed behavior in current `after` mode, and was deleted afterward. The final validation sequence now passes for the targeted persistence/migration suites, grouped-flow suites, the full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest sweep, and the filtered TypeScript gate for `src/tools/video-editor`. Checkpointed the result to `.megaplan/plans/finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628/execution_batch_5.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: rowsToConfig now applies a shared 4-decimal timing policy and derives hold durations from one rounded start/end pair so rematerialization keeps grouped interior boundaries stable. Added a repeated round-trip regression that keeps a 2.3334 boundary exact across six configToRows/rowsToConfig cycles with no gap or overlap. Verified with full-file runs of `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts` and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.test.ts`.

TimelineCanvas now routes `onLostPointerCapture` through the existing `endResize()` teardown, so the same cleanup path clears overrides, releases capture, decrements pending ops, resets `interactionStateRef.resize`, and notifies idle listeners. Added a lost-capture regression that proves preview width snaps back to committed rows, `onActionResizeEnd` fires with the previewed bounds, and the interaction-end listener runs. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and the combined targeted sweep.

Removed the deprecated `PinnedShotGroup.start` and `PinnedShotGroup.children` fields from the exported type, added optional snapshot `start`/`end` bounds for later image restore work, and kept legacy compatibility contained to `repairConfig()` via a local legacy cast. Migration now rewrites projection-shaped groups to ordered soft-tag `clipIds` only while preserving deep-cloned snapshot data, and serialization tests assert legacy fields are never re-emitted. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts` and the combined targeted sweep.

`usePinnedShotGroups.ts` now pins and syncs groups as ordered soft-tag `clipIds` only, diffing `Shot.images[]` against live row actions, reusing/adding/removing row actions directly, and rescheduling sync while interaction is active. Updated `useShotGroups.test.ts`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, and the pinned-group sync assertions in `useClipEditing.test.ts` to assert live-row-derived membership instead of legacy projection fields. Verified with two full-file sweeps of `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`; the only remaining failures are the two untouched `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests in `useClipEditing.test.ts`.

`useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` now snapshots image clips with absolute `start/end` bounds, switches groups to `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, and restores image rows from the snapshot without reading or writing legacy `start`/`children`. Updated the switch-to-video / switch-to-images coverage in `useClipEditing.test.ts` to assert soft-tag group state plus snapshot bounds. Verified inside the same two full-file sweeps; the updated T5 assertions pass, and the only remaining failures are the two untouched `useTimelineCommit` reconciliation tests elsewhere in `useClipEditing.test.ts`.

`useExternalDrop.ts` now creates shot row actions and the pinned group in one `rows` commit using ordered soft-tag `clipIds`, with no projection-child construction. Updated `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` to assert the actual row/meta/group result. Verified with two full-file sweeps of the batch target suites; `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` passed cleanly in both runs.

`useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` no longer uses `moveGroup()` at the remaining grouped move callsites. Grouped cross-track moves now translate live rows, patch each member’s `track` meta, update clip order, and rewrite the group’s `trackId` plus ordered `clipIds` atomically, including the create-new-track path. Updated `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to assert the new commit shapes. Verified with two full-file sweeps of the batch target suites; `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed cleanly in both runs.

Deleted the five deprecated compatibility exports from `pinned-group-projection.ts`, rewrote `timeline-save-utils.test.ts` around soft-tag `clipIds`, and removed the last legacy `start`/`children` fixture inputs from `useClipEditing.test.ts`. Verified with an exact symbol/tag grep over `src/tools/video-editor` that returns no matches for the deleted exports, legacy `PinnedShotGroup` fields, or listed diagnostic log tags, then ran `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`, which passed cleanly (5 files, 44 tests).

Created a throwaway repro test under `src/tools/video-editor`, ran it in `REPRO_MODE=before` to reproduce both the grouped-boundary drift and lost-pointer-capture stuck-preview symptoms, then ran it in `REPRO_MODE=after` to confirm the fixed behavior and deleted it afterward. Final verification passed on the accepted sequence: targeted suites (`migrate`, `serialize`, `timeline-save-utils`, `render-bounds.validation`), grouped-flow suites (`TimelineCanvas`, `useShotGroups`, `useClipDrag`, `useClipEditing`, `useExternalDrop`, `useTimelineTrackManagement`), the full `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor` sweep (40 files, 210 tests), and the filtered TypeScript gate (`npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit --pretty false 2>&1 | rg '^src/tools/video-editor'`) with no remaining `src/tools/video-editor` diagnostics.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The new regression serializes two adjacent grouped hold clips with a 2.3334 shared boundary through six full rematerialize cycles and asserts left end == right start on every cycle, with both clips preserving their original bounds.

The lost-capture regression now fires `lostpointercapture` after an active resize preview and confirms the shared `endResize()` path clears the preview, ends the session, flips `interactionStateRef.resize` back to false, releases capture, decrements pending ops, calls `onActionResizeEnd`, and triggers an idle listener once.

Migration and serialization coverage now loads a projection-shaped pinned group with legacy `start` and `children`, repairs it into ordered `clipIds` only, preserves snapshot bounds, and asserts both repaired and serialized output omit `start` and `children`.

`usePinnedGroupSync` now rebuilds image-mode group membership from `Shot.images[]` generation order plus live row actions, re-derives ordered `clipIds` from the committed rows, and reschedules itself until drag/resize interaction ends. The updated sync assertions in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts` and the live-row derivation checks in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts` cover that behavior.

The final-video switch path now snapshots each image clip’s absolute `start/end` bounds and commits `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, while the restore path rebuilds image rows from those snapshot bounds and restores the group’s image `clipIds`. Both directions are covered by the updated `useSwitchToFinalVideo` tests in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts`.

Dropping a shot now produces one `rows` edit containing the created actions, meta updates, clip-order override, and a soft-tag pinned group whose `clipIds` match the visible committed order. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` asserts that full result.

The remaining grouped cross-track move callsites now update rows, per-clip `track` meta, group `trackId`, and ordered `clipIds` atomically so the committed state matches the translated live group. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` covers move-to-row, create-track-and-move, and grouped arrow-key moves in both directions.

A precise grep over `src/tools/video-editor` now returns no matches for `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `moveGroup`, `projectPinnedShotGroups`, `PinnedShotGroup.start`, `PinnedShotGroup.children`, or the listed diagnostic log tags. The remaining soft-tag coverage in `timeline-save-utils.test.ts`, `serialize.test.ts`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, `useClipEditing.test.ts`, and `useShotGroups.test.ts` passes on the updated expectations.

The throwaway repro test reproduced the old drift and stuck-preview states in `REPRO_MODE=before`, then confirmed adjacency preservation and lost-capture cleanup in `REPRO_MODE=after`, and was deleted afterward. The final repo state passes the targeted suites, grouped-flow suites, the full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest run, and the filtered TypeScript gate with no remaining `src/tools/video-editor` diagnostics.
